title: forsteronia graciloides
intro: Forsteronia graciloides, also known as the "curare vine," is a plant species in the family Menispermaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antivenom effects, which are attributed to the presence of the alkaloid, curarine. The health benefits of Forsteronia graciloides include its potential to treat snake bites, wounds, and skin infections, as well as its use as a sedative and anesthetic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture, infusion, or decoction, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
